content:
{
  "title": "Syntax-Aware Prompting: Leveraging Abstract Syntax Trees for Improved Code Generation with LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often generate code that is functionally incorrect or syntactically invalid, particularly when handling non-trivial program structures. Current prompting methods do not sufficiently guide models to respect language syntax and structure throughout the generation process.",
  "motivation": "Code generation accuracy remains a limiting factor for LLM adoption in software development. Bridging the gap between natural language instructions and precise, syntactically correct code is crucial for automation and developer productivity. Prior work has not fully explored how explicit syntax guidance can be introduced into the LLM prompting pipeline.",
  "method": "We propose Syntax-Aware Prompting (SAP), a novel prompting framework that augments natural language instructions with explicit, partial Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) scaffolding. Prompts include placeholders for code elements (e.g., function signatures, control flow constructs) derived from parsed examples or specification, which the LLM must fill in. During inference, the model is guided to generate code consistent with the provided AST fragments, reducing syntactic and structural errors. The prompt format is language-agnostic and can incorporate intermediate verification steps, such as confirming subtree well-formedness prior to full code completion.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate SAP on HumanEval, MBPP, and competitive programming datasets. Baselines will include standard CoT prompting, structure-aware prompting (from SGLang and Structured CoT), and self-revision approaches. Metrics will cover syntactic correctness, functional correctness (pass@k), and structural fidelity to intended ASTs."
}
